articles.title: Person medflighted after falling from zoo ride in Mendon
articles.description:
Three people attempted to catch the man, but he still hit the ground "with considerable force," a witness says.

The post Person medflighted after falling from zoo ride in Mendon appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: A man fell from a zoo ride in Mendon and was medflighted to a hospital.
Witnesses reported three people attempted to catch him before he hit the ground.
The incident raises safety concerns for local amusement attractions in the Boston area.

articles.title: New details emerge as Mass. McDonald’s employee accused of giving her ex-girlfriend contaminated fries
articles.description:
Surveillance video shows Kaylie Santos apparently spitting into a french fry carton and shoving fries into her mouth before filling the container, police say.

The post New details emerge as Mass. McDonald’s employee accused of giving her ex-girlfriend contaminated fries appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Kaylie Santos, a McDonald's employee, allegedly contaminated fries for her ex-girlfriend.
Surveillance footage shows Santos spitting into a fry carton on May 18.
The incident raises health concerns for Boston residents regarding food safety.

articles.title: Americans among those affected by Ebola outbreak, CDC says
articles.description:
The World Health Organization declared the outbreak a global health emergency Saturday, a day after it was first announced by Africa’s top public health authority.

The post Americans among those affected by Ebola outbreak, CDC says appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: World Health Organization declared Ebola outbreak a global health emergency on May 17, 2026.
CDC reports Americans among those affected, raising concerns for Boston residents traveling abroad.
Local health officials may increase monitoring and preparedness in response to the outbreak.

articles.title: Odds are increasing for a ‘super El Niño’ this summer. Here’s what that means for Mass.
articles.description:
“What goes on in the tropical Pacific doesn't stay in the tropical Pacific."

The post Odds are increasing for a ‘super El Niño’ this summer. Here’s what that means for Mass. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Super El Niño expected to impact Massachusetts weather this summer.
Increased odds of a super El Niño reaching 70% by June 2024.
Potential for extreme weather patterns affecting Boston's summer temperatures and precipitation.
